DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT FRANCISCO BAY INN UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO INITIAL DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND OTHERWISE DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO FILE RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTIONS TO ALL SUCH REQUESTS AND PRODUCE ALL REQUESTED DOCUMENTS WITH 21 DAYS OF THE DATE OF ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILED TO IDENTIFY THE PERSON, PARTY, OR ATTORNEY AGAINST WHOM THE SANCTIONS WERE SOUGHT IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION, AS REQUIRED. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2023.040. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT JAMESHFLEMING@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMING; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2025 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-30-2024 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2024 Continued To Mar-03-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Outstanding Discovery Responses To Initial Discovery Requests And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FRANCISCO BAY INN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Outstanding Discovery Responses To Initial Discovery Requests And Request For Sanctions

The unopposed motion of Defendant Francisco Bay Inn to Compel Plaintiff's Outstanding Discovery Responses to Initial Discovery Requests is granted in part and otherwise denied. Plaintiff is ordered to file responses without objections to all such requests and produce all requested documents with 21 days of the date of order. Sanctions are denied because Defendant failed to identify the person, party, or attorney against whom the sanctions were sought in the notice of motion, as required. Code of Civil Procedure Section 2023.040.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-16-2023 Set For Trial On Jul-08-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
